data.title: mypy clean-up: auto-generated Alembic migration trips 37 'Module has no attribute sql' errors

data.body: Discovered during NAVI-6 review. 'uv run mypy .' reports ~37 errors, ALL in the auto-generated Alembic migration migrations/versions/939367c9ee06_*.py ('Module has no attribute "sql"'). Pre-existing, not introduced by NAVI-6 (broker.py + tests are mypy-clean). Fix options: add per-module mypy override (ignore_errors) for migrations/, or import sqlalchemy as sa correctly in the template. Low priority — does not affect runtime. Well-understood -> backlog.

REVIEW (cold, commit 0fea831, branch dev). Reviewed: plan + full diff (pyproject.toml only, 3 additive lines in [tool.mypy]: exclude = ["^migrations/"] + 2 comment lines). Ran all checks cold.

Command results:
- uv run mypy . -> 'Success: no issues found in 34 source files' (0 errors; was 37 'Module has no attribute sql' errors pre-fix)
- uv run mypy (bare) -> 'Success: no issues found in 17 source files' (still checks ALL src files)
- uv run ruff check . -> 'All checks passed!'
- uv run pytest -q -> 110 passed, 1 skipped (pre-existing NAVI_LIVE_TESTS network-gated skip), 0 failures

Acceptance criteria: (1) mypy . clean PASS; (2) bare mypy clean PASS; (3) ruff clean + pytest green PASS; (4) migration 939367c9ee06_initial_schema_spec_5.py absent from diff and present on disk, untouched PASS; (5) only pyproject.toml [tool.mypy] changed, no source/runtime change PASS.

Adversarial scrutiny: exclude is NOT over-broad — bare mypy still checks 17 src .py files (verified src has exactly 17 .py files), so no real type errors are hidden; only the migrations/ tree is skipped under recursive 'mypy .'. Excluded file is genuinely auto-generated boilerplate (header: 'commands auto generated by Alembic'), pure schema DDL (op.create_table/sa.Column), no hand-written logic — excluding is conventional and correct. No other pyproject.toml table disturbed (diff is 3 pure additions inside [tool.mypy]; pytest/ruff tables intact). Pre-fix parent config confirmed to lack exclude, so the change is load-bearing.

No findings. VERDICT: PASS
